items.text: GIVE BLOOD, GET A SHIRT! Campus Blood Drive. May 15-16, 10 AM-4 PM. Anderson Hall Lobby. All donors receive a free T-shirt and a $5 campus dining voucher. Walk-ins welcome. Bring ID. Sponsored by the Student Health Office.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what do donors receive?

options.A: A free meal at a campus restaurant
options.B: A parking pass for Anderson Hall
options.C: A health screening from the Student Health Office
options.D: A dining voucher and a complimentary shirt

questions.explanation: The flyer states donors receive 'a free T-shirt and a $5 campus dining voucher.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the blood drive?

options.A: Appointments are not strictly required.
options.B: It is only for students with valid health insurance.
options.C: It takes place over a single afternoon.
options.D: The dining voucher must be used on May 15.

questions.explanation: The flyer says 'Walk-ins welcome,' which implies people can participate without a prior appointment.

items.text: STUDENT THEATER PRESENTS: 'Midnight Library'! Shows: March 3-7 at 7:30 PM. University Theater, Room 204. Tickets: $5 for students, $10 general. Purchase at the door or online. Seating is limited. Directed by Leo Chen.

questions.stem: Where will the theater performances be held?

options.A: In the main campus library
options.B: At an off-campus venue
options.C: In Anderson Hall Lobby
options.D: In Room 204 of University Theater

questions.explanation: The flyer specifies the location as 'University Theater, Room 204.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about ticket availability?

options.A: It might be wise to buy tickets in advance.
options.B: Online tickets are more expensive than door tickets.
options.C: Only students are permitted to attend.
options.D: Tickets are free for university staff.

questions.explanation: The notice that 'Seating is limited' suggests tickets could sell out, making advance purchase a good idea.

items.text: Dear Biology 101 Students, This is a reminder for the mandatory lab safety training. It will be held this Friday at 2:00 PM in Science Building, Room 105. Please bring your student ID. Attendance will be recorded. Regards, Dr. Park

questions.stem: What are students instructed to bring to the training?

options.A: A completed safety waiver form
options.B: Their student identification card
options.C: Personal protective equipment
options.D: A textbook for Biology 101

questions.explanation: The email explicitly states, 'Please bring your student ID.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the training session?

options.A: It is optional for most students.
options.B: It will be led by a teaching assistant.
options.C: Student presence will be officially noted.
options.D: It will last for several hours.

questions.explanation: The email says 'Attendance will be recorded,' which means their presence will be officially noted.

items.text: Hi Sam, Thank you for volunteering as a campus tour guide. Your shift is confirmed for Saturday, April 10, from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM. Meet at the Welcome Center. You'll receive a $10 lunch stipend. Please confirm. Best, Ms. Chen

questions.stem: What time does Sam's volunteer shift begin?

options.A: At 8:00 in the morning
options.B: At 9:00 in the morning
options.C: At 10:00 in the morning
options.D: At 12:00 in the afternoon

questions.explanation: The email states the shift is 'from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM.'

questions.stem: What is Sam most likely expected to do after reading this email?

options.A: Purchase a uniform for the tour.
options.B: Reply to confirm the shift details.
options.C: Go directly to the Welcome Center on Saturday.
options.D: Collect the lunch stipend in advance.

questions.explanation: The email ends with the instruction 'Please confirm,' implying Sam should reply.

items.text: NOTICE: The South Lot B parking area will be closed for repaving from May 15 to May 17. Alternative parking is available in the North Garage. All vehicles must be removed by 7:00 PM on May 14.

questions.stem: Where should drivers park during the closure?

options.A: In the adjacent South Lot A
options.B: At the Anderson Hall visitor lot
options.C: In the North Garage
options.D: On any campus street

questions.explanation: The notice directly states 'Alternative parking is available in the North Garage.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the notice?

options.A: The work will be completed early.
options.B: The lot will be inaccessible for multiple days.
options.C: Only faculty cars need to be moved.
options.D: The repaving was scheduled by students.

questions.explanation: The closure dates 'from May 15 to May 17' indicate a multi-day period where the lot cannot be used.

items.text: LIBRARY HOURS ADJUSTMENT: During Spring Break, March 11-15, the main library will operate from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily. Regular hours resume on Monday, March 18. The 24-hour study room in Anderson Hall will remain accessible.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when do regular library hours resume?

options.A: On Sunday, March 17
options.B: At the end of Spring Break
options.C: After the 24-hour room closes
options.D: On Monday, March 18

questions.explanation: The notice clearly states 'Regular hours resume on Monday, March 18.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the library's Spring Break schedule?

options.A: It is more convenient for most students.
options.B: The building is open for fewer hours.
options.C: All study spaces will be closed.
options.D: No librarians will be present.

questions.explanation: The notice specifies reduced hours ('10:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily') compared to typical, longer library hours.

items.text: NETWORK MAINTENANCE: Campus Wi-Fi will be unavailable in all academic buildings from 11:00 PM on Saturday, May 4, until 5:00 AM on Sunday, May 5, for essential system upgrades. Please plan your work accordingly. We apologize for the inconvenience.

questions.stem: When will the Wi-Fi service interruption begin?

options.A: At 11:00 PM on Saturday
options.B: Early on Sunday morning
options.C: Throughout the weekend
options.D: During daytime hours on May 5

questions.explanation: The notice specifies the Wi-Fi will be unavailable 'from 11:00 PM on Saturday, May 4.'

questions.stem: The word 'essential' in the notice is closest in meaning to

options.A: necessary
options.B: complicated
options.C: optional
options.D: experimental

questions.explanation: In this context, 'essential' describes upgrades that are crucial or required, making 'necessary' the best synonym.

items.text: Due to road construction, the Green Line campus shuttle will be rerouted from May 15-19. Stops at Anderson Hall and South Lot B are temporarily suspended. A temporary stop will be established at the Union Building. A $5 late fee applies for parking pass adjustments not made by Friday.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is a consequence of the shuttle detour?

options.A: To receive a refund for the shuttle pass
options.B: To use a temporary stop at the Union Building
options.C: To avoid a parking ticket at Anderson Hall
options.D: To pay a higher fare for the Green Line

questions.explanation: The notice states a temporary stop will be established at the Union Building due to the rerouting.

questions.stem: What specific action must be taken by Friday to avoid a penalty?

options.A: To adjust parking passes for a fee waiver
options.B: To register for an alternative shuttle route
options.C: To submit a construction complaint form
options.D: To confirm attendance at a facilities meeting

questions.explanation: The notice indicates a $5 late fee applies for parking pass adjustments not made by Friday.

items.text: Dear Student, Your meal plan has a remaining balance of $42.75 for this semester. To receive a refund, please complete the online form by March 10. Refunds will be processed within 10 business days. Sincerely, Ms. Chen, Dining Services

questions.stem: What is the stated purpose of the online form?

options.A: To request a refund for a meal plan balance
options.B: To renew the meal plan for the next semester
options.C: To report an issue with dining service quality
options.D: To check the current balance on the card

questions.explanation: The email says to complete the online form to receive a refund for the remaining balance.

questions.stem: According to the email, by when must the form be submitted?

options.A: Within 10 business days of receipt
options.B: Before the end of the current semester
options.C: By the specified date of March 10
options.D: After the balance is confirmed by staff

questions.explanation: The email specifies the form must be completed by March 10.

items.text: Just tried the new iced matcha latte at Campus Grounds! It's only $4.50 and so refreshing for studying. Perfect for this warm weather. They have it all week until 8 PM. You've got to try it!

questions.stem: What specific detail is given about the new drink?

options.A: Its price is four dollars and fifty cents.
options.B: It is a hot beverage for cold days.
options.C: It is available exclusively on weekends.
options.D: Its main ingredient is coffee.

questions.explanation: The post states the iced matcha latte is 'only $4.50'.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the person who wrote the post?

options.A: They work at the Campus Grounds café.
options.B: They are dissatisfied with the drink's quality.
options.C: They enjoyed the drink they purchased.
options.D: They prefer to study in the library.

questions.explanation: The post uses positive language like 'so refreshing' and 'You've got to try it!', implying the writer enjoyed it.

items.text: Hi Alex, This confirms your tutoring appointment with Dr. Park for Thursday at 3:00 PM. Please meet in Room 204 of the Science Building. Remember to bring your textbook and notes. There's a $10 fee for cancellations with less than 24 hours' notice. Best, Tutoring Center

questions.stem: Where should Alex go for the confirmed appointment?

options.A: To the main library study room
options.B: To the front desk of the Tutoring Center
options.C: To Dr. Park's office in Anderson Hall
options.D: To Room 204 of the Science Building

questions.explanation: The email instructs Alex to meet in Room 204 of the Science Building.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the cancellation policy?

options.A: All cancellations require a doctor's note.
options.B: A fee is charged for last-minute cancellations.
options.C: Appointments can be rescheduled for free anytime.
options.D: The fee is refundable if you rebook later.

questions.explanation: The email mentions a $10 fee for cancellations with less than 24 hours' notice, implying a charge for last-minute changes.

items.text: Notice: HVAC maintenance in Dalton Hall is scheduled for April 5-7. During this period, room temperatures may fluctuate. Occupants are advised to dress in layers. For urgent issues, contact the Facilities Office at ext. 5555. We apologize for the inconvenience.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should occupants do during the maintenance period?

options.A: To vacate the building entirely for three days
options.B: To report all temperature changes immediately
options.C: To schedule their own repair appointments
options.D: To wear adaptable clothing like layers

questions.explanation: The notice advises occupants to dress in layers due to potential temperature fluctuations.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the nature of the upcoming work?

options.A: It will completely replace the building's heating system.
options.B: It might cause temporary discomfort for building users.
options.C: It is expected to lower everyone's utility bills.
options.D: It requires all classes in Dalton Hall to be canceled.

questions.explanation: The notice warns of temperature fluctuations and apologizes for inconvenience, implying possible temporary discomfort.
